description: Unified scoring matrix for YouTube long-form content opportunities. Adapted from content-research viral signal matrix for YouTube-specific factors.

factors.name: Opportunity
factors.description: Search demand vs competition. Combines real search volume (Google Keyword Planner) with YouTube competition analysis.

scoring.5: Volume 10K+/mo AND competition low (small channels, old content, low saturation)
scoring.4: Volume 5-10K/mo AND competition medium OR Volume 10K+ with moderate competition
scoring.3: Volume 1-5K/mo AND competition medium — viable but crowded
scoring.2: Volume 500-1K/mo OR competition high (large channels dominate top 3)
scoring.1: Volume <500/mo OR competition very high (saturated, huge channels only)

data_sources.volume: Apify iskander/google-keyword-search-volume-api — real Google Keyword Planner data
data_sources.competition: Apify streamers/youtube-scraper — top 3 videos per keyword: saturation %, avg channel subs, freshness gap

factors.name: Ranking Probability
factors.description: Jay's specific likelihood to rank for this keyword based on channel authority, existing content, and audience engagement.

scoring.5: Existing content on exact topic + search terms already driving views + engagement >3% + smaller channels than Jay rank for it
scoring.4: Adjacent content exists + related search terms drive traffic + engagement 2-3%
scoring.3: No existing content but topic aligns with pillar + engagement >2% on related content
scoring.2: New topic area, engagement 1-2%, would require building authority from scratch
scoring.1: Off-pillar topic, engagement <1%, dominated by channels 10x+ Jay's size

data_sources.topical_authority: YouTube Analytics MCP get_channel_videos — does Jay have videos on this topic?
data_sources.search_authority: YouTube Analytics MCP get_search_terms — do related search terms drive views?
data_sources.engagement: YouTube Analytics MCP get_engagement_metrics — does Jay's audience engage with related content?
data_sources.size_match: Compare Jay's subscriber count vs keyword competitors' subscriber counts

factors.name: Trend Momentum
factors.description: Growth trajectory based on competitor video velocity and content recency. Higher weight in timely phase.

scoring.5: Competitor video at 5x+ velocity in last 7 days — topic is exploding NOW
scoring.4: Multiple competitor videos on topic in last 14 days with above-average performance
scoring.3: Stable evergreen topic — consistent search demand, no surge or decline
scoring.2: Declining interest — fewer recent videos, lower views on recent uploads
scoring.1: Dead topic — no recent competitor content, trend clearly over

data_sources.velocity: Apify streamers/youtube-scraper — recent competitor videos, views/day vs channel average
data_sources.recency: Upload dates of top-ranking videos — old top results = opportunity

factors.name: Content Fit
factors.description: Alignment to Jay's content pillars and audience interests.

scoring.5: Direct pillar match: cold email setup, AI automation tutorial, lead gen strategy
scoring.4: Adjacent pillar: email marketing, sales tools, LinkedIn outreach, CRM
scoring.3: Cross-pillar: touches 2+ pillars tangentially, could angle into niche
scoring.2: Stretch: general business/AI topic requiring significant reframing
scoring.1: Off-topic: no clear connection to Jay's audience or expertise

make_it.label: MAKE IT
make_it.action: Ideal opportunity — research and script immediately

watch.label: WATCH
watch.action: Good potential — monitor and revisit in 1 week

skip.label: SKIP
skip.action: Not enough opportunity — pass for now

unicorn_scoring.description: How to score competitor unicorn videos (10x+ outliers) on the same matrix
unicorn_scoring.opportunity: Based on the topic's search volume + how many other videos exist on this exact angle
unicorn_scoring.ranking: If a competitor proved the topic works, Jay can likely rank too — score 3-5 based on channel size comparison
unicorn_scoring.trend: Based on video's velocity (views/day) — unicorn status implies high trend score

velocity_thresholds.description: For Phase 2 timely research — views per day relative to channel average
